vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is partially inaccurate. P1 (String of Nickels) is absent/bare soil. P2/P4 (Mexican Mint/Silver Guest) are not clearly distinguishable as separate entities; a single, struggling specimen remains. P3 (Pothos) is present with the anchor.

plant_audit.P1: Yellow pot contains only soil and a plant marker. No visible foliage.
plant_audit.P2: Black pot contains a single, thin, chlorotic stem with minimal leaf structure. High risk of total senescence.
plant_audit.P3: Black pot contains a single, healthy-looking Pothos leaf and the white rabbit anchor. Soil appears moist.
plant_audit.P4: Not identified as a distinct specimen; likely subsumed or lost.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, appears damp. No fungal blooms detected.
biome_observations.debris: Eggshell fragments present in P2/P4 pot, likely an intervention for calcium or pH adjustment.
biome_observations.desk_surface: Clean, though a cable/wire has been introduced near the yellow pot in later images.

temporal_deltas.earliest_to_t4: P2/P4 specimen shows progressive wilting and loss of turgor pressure.
temporal_deltas.t4_to_current: The specimen in P2/P4 has undergone a postural collapse, shifting from a semi-upright position to a recumbent state against the soil surface.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: P3 is the only stable element. P2 is in a state of terminal decline, likely due to light deficiency or root-zone issues. P1 is effectively a dormant or failed container.
anomalies: Cable/wire proximity in later images (New Intervention)
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is in a state of decline. While the Pothos (P3) maintains structural integrity, the remaining specimens are failing. The presence of eggshells suggests an attempt at soil amendment, but the visual evidence indicates the plant in the black pot is unable to recover. The yellow pot (P1) is currently non-functional as a botanical host.
